stories.title: Ukraine steps up attacks on Russia’s Black Sea fuel network
stories.summary: Ukraine struck 12 vessels belonging to Russia’s shadow fleet in the Black Sea on Thursday night, the commander of the Unmanned Systems Forces, Robert Brovdi, announced on Telegram. The attacks aim to prevent Russia from supplying fuel to the occupied regions of Eastern Ukraine and Crimea and curb its effort to circumvent sanctions, Brovdi explained. “The goal is incurable paralysis of oil, fuel, cargo logistics in circumvention of sanctions,” the commander wrote . Overall, 159

stories.title: Taras Kachka named Ukraine’s new ambassador to the EU
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Taras Kachka, Ukraine’s former deputy prime minister in charge of European integration, has been named as Kyiv’s new ambassador to the European Union, President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced on Friday . Kachka takes over from Vsevolod Chentsov, who will take up Kachka’s previous role as deputy prime minister in charge of integration with the EU in a role-swap. The switch forms part of a wider wartime reshuffle that has recast several senior portfolios, following

stories.title: Ukraine: Several killed and injured in Russian strikes
stories.summary: Russia carried out deadly strikes across Ukraine overnight that hit residential areas, while Ukraine said drone strikes in the Black Sea hit 12 Russian vessels.

stories.title: Weather tracker: Thunderstorms strike across Europe amid record heatwave
stories.summary: Storms are typical during intense heat but this week’s have been extreme. Plus, deadly monsoon rains in Bangladesh Hailstones the size of golf balls have been seen in French villages as, on top of the exceptional European heatwave, thunderstorms have struck across parts of Europe. While thunderstorms are typical during and after a period of extreme heat, the storms across countries such as France, Germany and Poland have been particularly severe, bringing flooding, strong winds and heavy showers

stories.title: CDC and FDA link lettuce at Taco Bell in five states to cyclospora outbreak
stories.summary: FDA investigation identified a single supplier of the lettuce, but federal warnings did not name the company Federal health officials have identified lettuce from Mexico served by Taco Bell locations across five US states as a source of the widespread outbreak of diarrhea-causing parasite cyclospora . The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) late Thursday confirmed the source and warned consumers not to eat shredded iceberg lettuce from Taco Bell restaurants in Indiana, Kentucky, Mic

stories.title: Taco Bell removes lettuce from menu in US after links to explosive diarrhoea
stories.summary: Lettuce from one supplier is being removed indefinitely "out of an abundance of caution", the company says.

stories.title: Recalling the joys of learning languages | Letters
stories.summary: Readers respond to an article by Rhiannon Lucy Cosslett and note that a willingness to ‘act the goat’ is the greatest predictor of success How refreshing to read Rhiannon Lucy Cosslett’s piece on the advantages and fun of learning languages ( At last, a proper excuse for monoglots to learn another language: it helps keep your brain young, 12 July ). I first heard something similar to the expression she mentions from my future French father-in-law when he opened a very good bordeaux with the comm

stories.title: Air quality plummets in 20 US states as smoke from Canadian wildfires spreads
stories.summary: Millions of Americans face air quality alerts from Minnesota to New York as authorities urge people to stay indoors Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email Tell us: have you been affected by the spread of wildfire smoke in the US and Canada? Smoke from wildfires burning in south-central Canada and parts of Minnesota is spreading across the US, prompting air quality alerts in more than 20 states with millions of Americans expected to face unhealthy air conditions this week. The smoke fr

stories.title: Merkely Béla nem szólt, hogy tulajdonos volt a fia cégében, amikor az az általa vezetett egyetemmel közösen százmilliós tételben nyert közpénzt
stories.summary: A KinaviX projektje Hankó Balázs egyedi döntésével kapott háromszor is támogatást, hol a Semmelweis, hol a cég neve alatt, hol közösen. A rektor az egyetemi alapítvány kuratóriumi tagja, így már tavaly jeleznie kellett volna az összeférhetetlenséget, mikor az egyeteme a céggel szerződött. Már kiszállt a KinaviX-ból, de részesedését a Semmelweis cége vette meg – amelynek szintén ő az egyik vezetője.

stories.title: Gajdos László: Egyszerűen nem találjuk az előző kormány 40 milliárdos velencei-tavi mentési tervét
stories.summary: Az élő környezetért felelős miniszter a Magyar Hangnak azt mondta: mostantól nem lehet majd semmilyen beruházást végrehajtani úgy, hogy nem veszik figyelembe a környezetvédelmi szempontokat.

stories.title: Lesérült a forgatáson a God of War-sorozat főhőse, új színészt keresnek a helyére
stories.summary: Újra kell forgatni az eddigi jeleneteket is.

stories.title: Jeszenszky Géza: Szijjártó Péter elképesztően hiú ember, aki körülvette magát fullajtárokkal
stories.summary: A volt külügyminiszter szerint a BYD-hez igazolt Szijjártó számára nincs visszaút a magyar politikába.

stories.title: Vért izzadt a fotós, amikor a Lamine Yamalt fürdető Messiről kellett képet készítenie
stories.summary: Joan Monfort szerint Yamal anyja oldotta a feszültséget a 2007-es fotózáson. Yamal és Messi a vb-döntőben találkozik egymással először ellenfélként.

stories.title: Öt drága hiba, amit ne kövess el a bankolásban nyaralás alatt!
stories.summary: Gondtalan pihenés után méregdrága meglepetés a bankszámlakivonaton? A külföldi nyaralás alatt elkövetett apró, rutinszerűnek tűnő bankolási hibák akár a teljes utazási költség 10-15 százalékát is felemészthetik. Összeszedtük, mivel szoktuk a legjobban növelni a kiadásainkat szinte észrevétlenül. Megmutatjuk azt is, mit érdemes csinálni helyette.

stories.title: Varga Mihály és Kármán András tőzsdét fejlesztene, adódik pár alacsonyan csüngő gyümölcs
stories.summary: A magyar piacot versenyhátrányba taszító tranzakciós illeték eltörlése és a kínálat bővítése biztosan segítene, utóbbiban az MVM régóta ígért tőzsdére vitele lenne a nagy dobás.

stories.title: Orbán Balázs szerint senki nem akart a Fidesz kampányfőnöke lenni, így esett végül rá a választás
stories.summary: A Fidesz történelmi vereséghez vezető kampányának kampányfőnöke arról is beszélt a Különutas YouTube-csatornának adott interjújában, hogy mire őt felkérték a pozícióra, már eldőlt a kampánystratégia.

stories.title: Gyereket akar, párkapcsolatot nem
stories.summary: Gyerekvállalás, nők helyzete a mai Japánban, testképzavarok, a férfivilág elvárásai. A japán Kavakami Mieko magyarul megjelent regényében tabutémákat feszeget.

stories.title: Komolyabb jogokat kaptak volna az élettársi kapcsolatban élők Lengyelországban, a meleg párok miatt az államfő megvétózta
stories.summary: Az elnöki kabinetfőnök szerint Nawrocki akkor fogja aláírni a törvényt, ha az nem ássa alá a férfi és nő közötti kapcsolatként felfogott házasság alkotmányos védelmét.

stories.title: Kevésbé voltak pontosak a vonatok júniusban
stories.summary: A MÁV a rendkívüli hőséggel és a megnövekedett utasszámmal magyarázza az érték romlását.

stories.title: A fosszilis energia visszaszorításával csökkentene rezsit az EU, de puhítana egy ezt segítő rendszeren

stories.title: Megvan az alku, az Uberé lesz a Foodorát is birtokló német vállalat
stories.summary: Ételkiszállítóként is megjelenik Magyarországon az Uber a Foodorán keresztül.

stories.summary: Hermányi Zsolt eddig a kórház vezető főorvosa volt.

stories.title: Teenager accused of carrying out sabotage actions in Poland for Russia
stories.summary: Polish security services say the aim was to incite ethnic tensions between Poland and Ukraine.

stories.title: Google mega fine to slash taxpayers’ contributions to Brussels
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — U.S. tech giant Google will inadvertently help fund the EU’s coffers as the European Commission plans to put its multibillion-euro fine toward the bloc’s central budget. The €4.6 billion sanction issued to Google in 2018, which is the EU’s largest-ever single fine, will go some way toward filling countries’ spending needs for 2026, a Commission official confirmed to POLITICO. The EU budget, which is made up predominantly of government contributions, is used to finance the

stories.title: EU loosens carbon market rules to let industry pollute for longer
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The European Commission has confirmed it will allow EU industry to continue emitting planet-warming gases well into the 2040s and offer more free pollution permits for longer, as part of an overhaul of the bloc’s Emissions Trading System. The Commission released the long-awaited revision on Friday after a full night of talks, following internal division over the details of the sweeping reforms to the EU’s most powerful tool for tackling climate change. The final proposal r

stories.title: Marines board tanker amid blockade of Iranian ports as US expands strikes with attacks on bridges
stories.summary: Boarding of M/T Wen Yao in Gulf of Oman comes as expanded airstrike campaign hits five bridges in southern Iran American forces boarded a ship in the Gulf of Oman on Thursday as part of the renewed blockade of Iran’s ports that began earlier this week, the US military said. US Marines boarded the M/T Wen Yao “to ensure full compliance with the ongoing US naval blockade,” US Central Command (Centcom) said in a post on X. Continue reading...

stories.title: ICE arrests human rights lawyer who fled Chinese crackdown
stories.summary: Arrest in Pennsylvania of Wu Shaoping, who is awaiting asylum decision, raises fears of deportation and persecution A Chinese human rights lawyer has been arrested by US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), raising concerns he could be deported to China where he would face persecution. Wu Shaoping fled China at the end of 2019 amid a crackdown on human rights lawyers. He travelled to the US on a tourist visa and made an asylum claim in 2020, for which he is still awaiting a decision. Conti

stories.title: Trump launches wide-ranging and unverified attack on integrity of US electoral system | First Thing
stories.summary: US president accused China of meddling in his 2020 election defeat and said system was ‘catastrophically’ unfair. Plus Christopher Nolan’s epic take on The Odyssey hits the big screen Don’t already get First Thing in your inbox? Sign up here Good morning. stories.title: Andy Burnham becomes Labour leader in final step towards becoming UK PM
stories.summary: Former Greater Manchester mayor says he is ready to beat the ‘new right’ and give power back to ‘the place where you live’ UK politics live – latest updates Andy Burnham has declared himself ready to “beat Britain’s new right” with a fresh, united approach as he becomes the leader of the Labour party before taking over as British prime minister on Monday. The former mayor of Greater Manchester won the overwhelming support of MPs, trade unions and party branches, making him the only choice to tak

stories.title: Seven Americans quarantining at Kenya Ebola facility after US travel ban, says aid group
stories.summary: Aid workers are first known people to quarantine at facility, which sparked huge opposition in Kenya Seven American aid workers who had been in Congo to fight the Ebola outbreak are quarantining at a new isolation facility in ⁠Kenya after the US government introduced travel ⁠restrictions, the head of a US charity ​employing them told Reuters. The aid workers are the first known people to quarantine at the facility, which has sparked huge opposition in Kenya and is at the heart of a legal case in

stories.title: Germany news: Macron and Merz lead bilateral cabinet talks
stories.summary: Berlin and Paris are seeking to deepen security cooperation as ministers meet in western Germany. Meanwhile, a court has delivered its verdict on an alleged supporter of the far-right NSU terror group. DW has more.

stories.title: Iran reports fresh strikes near Qeshm Island and accuses US over ‘barbaric’ hospital attack – as it happened
stories.summary: This live blog has been paused The Lebanese foreign minister, Youssef Raggi, said Lebanon has made a decision to “end Hezbollah’s military presence” and that decisions on war and foreign policy are the “exclusive prerogative of the Lebanese state”. The Lebanese government is pushing to disarm Hezbollah, one of the most heavily armed militias in the Middle East, and it has become a central component of the US-brokered talks between Lebanon and Israel. Lebanon has made its choice: there will be no

stories.title: Political crisis and protests in Ukraine as Zelenskyy defends sacking defence minister
stories.summary: President says he had to choose ‘one side or the other’ after breakdown of relations between ministry and military leaders Ukraine’s president, Volodymyr Zelenskyy, has defended his decision to dismiss the country’s popular defence minister, Mykhailo Fedorov , and confirmed reports that relations had broken down between the ministry and the country’s top army leadership. Speaking at a press conference in Kyiv with the British prime minister, Keir Starmer, Zelenskyy said there had been a “challen

stories.title: Trump rails against election systems — and familiar enemies
stories.summary: President Donald Trump on Thursday announced his administration is declassifying documents that he says outline vulnerabilities in U.S. election systems. In his 25-minute speech from the White House, Trump described efforts by China to access U.S. voter rolls and offered details about long-studied vulnerabilities in electronic voting machines. But none of the information Trump described appears to support his long-running claims that the 2020 election was stolen or

stories.title: EU border chaos feared at Dover crossing as busiest summer weekend looms
stories.summary: British domestic holidays are being pushed to their highest levels since Covid The start of the peak summer season is set to bring millions of drivers on to British roads, with concerns of traffic chaos as the port of Dover faces its biggest test yet of new EU border controls. The semi-functioning entry-exit system (EES) is credited, along with the heatwaves and fears about flights after the war in Iran , with helping push British domestic holidays to its highest levels since Covid halted intern

stories.title: Democrats criticize Trump’s claims of election interference – US politics live

During his speech, Trump also tried to unveil

stories.summary: Southern rail station also attacked, Iranian media says, as IRGC claims to have destroyed US fighter jets stationed in Jordan US marines board tanker amid blockade of Iranian ports The IRGC has threatened “more crushing” attacks against neighbouring countries hosting US bases, warning that they will pay a “devastating price” if American forces continue to attack Iran’s civilian infrastructure. In a statement carried by state media, it said: The American enemy and the hosts of its bases in the re

stories.title: Outrage at sacking of Ukraine’s defence minister overshadows Starmer’s final visit as PM – as it happened

stories.title: Xi Jinping calls for international cooperation in AI development at WAIC in Shanghai
stories.summary: Xi Jinping marked his first in-person attendance at the World ​Artificial Intelligence Conference (WAIC) in Shanghai, where he shared China's vision of AI diplomacy to challenge US dominance in the sector.

stories.title: India news: Modi silent as Sonam Wangchuk hunger strike enters 20th day
stories.summary: As Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike entered its 20th day and the "Cockroach" party continued its protest, Prime Minister Narendra Modi faced criticism for his reluctance to hold press conferences. DW has more.

stories.title: Mandela Day: Has South Africa failed his vision?
stories.summary: Three decades after apartheid, many South Africans say Nelson Mandela's vision remains unfulfilled. Inequality, xenophobia and anti-migrant protests continue to test his legacy.

stories.title: Iran war: US hits bridges as Tehran retaliates across region
stories.summary: The US launched a sixth night of strikes on Iran, with Tehran saying attacks on bridges killed several people. Iran has fired retaliatory strikes on US targets across the region. DW has more.

stories.title: Germany massively expands federal police powers
stories.summary: The German government has increased the powers of its federal police, especially in the use of AI, surveillance and drone defenses. But experts warn of constitutional challenges.

stories.title: Iran accuses US of hitting civilian infrastructure
stories.summary: The US military says its attacks were intended to "further degrade Iranian military capabilities".

stories.title: Zelensky's removal of popular defence minister sparks protests in Ukraine
stories.summary: A rift between Fedorov and Commander-in-Chief Oleksandr Syrskyi was apparently at the heart of Zelensky's decision.

stories.title: Andy Burnham crowned UK Labour leader — live updates
stories.summary: Andy Burnham is now the leader of Britain’s governing party, and is set to replace Keir Starmer as British prime minister early next week. At a special conference in London, the Makerfield MP — who returned to parliament last month after nine years as Greater Manchester mayor — was elected unopposed as Labour’s next leader, and pitched his incoming premiership as marking the biggest shift in British politics for 40 years. No other Labour MP received the required no

stories.title: Moroccan intelligence insider reveals widespread use of Pegasus hacking software
stories.summary: Whistleblower suggests internal security services deployed spyware from 2017 against key domestic and foreign targets A former member of Morocco’s domestic intelligence service has helped to provide an unprecedented insight into how the north African state used hacking software – including Pegasus spyware – to target journalists, human rights defenders, French politicians and Spanish cabinet ministers and police officers. Pegasus, which is manufactured by the Israel-based NSO Group, allows its o

stories.title: Pauline Hanson laments end of White Australia policy and talks up daughter’s leadership potential
stories.summary: Appearing on a podcast with the far-right UK activist who goes by the name Tommy Robinson, Hanson makes evidence-free claims about migrants Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Pauline Hanson has used an interview with the far-right British activist Tommy Robinson to blame the end of the White Australia policy for creating what she sees as problems with migration and said her daughter, Lee Hanson, was the future of On

stories.title: Cyclist Rohan Dennis pleads guilty to driving with suspended licence after crash that killed wife
stories.summary: Adelaide-based Olympian was banned from driving for five years over Melissa Hoskins’ 2023 death Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Former professional cyclist Rohan Dennis faces a jail sentence after admitting he drove while disqualified over the events leading up to the death of his Olympian wife, Melissa Hoskins. A judge had previously warned the former Olympian he would be jailed if he drove while banned. Continu

stories.title: China ‘strongly dissatisfied’ with nationalisation of British Steel
stories.summary: Move dealt ‘severe blow to Chinese companies’ confidence in investing in the UK’, says Ministry of Commerce China’s government has said it is “strongly dissatisfied” with the decision to nationalise British Steel this week, 15 months after the UK government intervened to prevent the closure of its steelworks in Scunthorpe and the loss of 4,000 jobs. On Thursday, British Steel was brought under public ownership to protect “the future of steel production”, the government announced. Continue readin

stories.title: Moira Deeming disendorsed as Victorian Liberal party candidate for upcoming state election
stories.summary: The party’s state executive decided MP’s fate after she withdrew her legal action against the party earlier this week Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Victorian Liberal MP Moira Deeming has been disendorsed ahead of the upcoming state election, despite dropping an eleventh-hour legal challenge against her own party. The state party called a meeting for Friday afternoon where they voted to remove the MP as a candidate for the November election. Continue reading...

stories.title: Lindsey Graham’s death leaves South Carolina confronting complex legacy: ‘You loved him and you hated him’
stories.summary: The US state mourns its longest-serving senator while Republicans scramble to choose a successor The South Carolina state house is a microcosm of the US’s contradictions. Outside there are memorials to the Confederate war dead and African American history. Below a statue of Strom Thurmond, a longtime US senator and racial segregationist, are the names of his five children including Essie Mae , whose mother, a Black maid, was 15 when Thurmond impregnated her. Thurmond died at the age of 100 in 20

stories.title: ‘I don’t play around’: Nolan fans fly into London to see The Odyssey at BFI Imax
stories.summary: Film fanatics arrive from US, Switzerland and Ireland for midnight premiere of director’s critically acclaimed epic Odysseus made his name by embarking on a perilous journey from Troy to Ithaca, plus a few unplanned diversions courtesy of the gods. But this is nothing on Christian Campbell, who last night travelled more than 4,000 miles to see the Greek king’s epic fable on the big screen. The 22-year-old film graduate, who aspires to be an editor, made the journey from Atlanta to London to watc

stories.title: Jackdaw gasfield would create only 27 full-time jobs, green campaigners say
stories.summary: Anti fossil-fuel groups are urging Andy Burnham to rule out new drilling, which they say will not shave a penny off bills More people can fit on to the top deck of a standard London bus than will be directly employed on the new Jackdaw gasfield in the North Sea, industry documents show. Only 27 direct full-time jobs would be created by Jackdaw, one of the biggest gasfields remaining in the North Sea , according to an environmental impact assessment filed publicly by its owner, Adura, a joint ven

stories.title: South East Water warns over survival as funds dry up
stories.summary: Supplier to 2.4m customers says it doesn’t have enough money to last beyond July 2027 South East Water has warned that there is “material uncertainty” over its survival, after a disastrous year in which the lossmaking company paid millions of pounds in fines and its chief executive was forced out. The water supplier to 2.4 million customers said it had sufficient funds to make it through to July 2027. However, “shortly after” it will need “new loan facilities in order to continue as a going conc

stories.title: Burnham calls for party unity and ‘new politics’ in first speech as Labour leader – UK politics live
stories.summary: Burnham criticises ‘decades of neoliberalism’ and says he wants to give people ‘hope back’ When Andy Burnham first tried to return to the Commons, by applying to be Labour’s candidate in the Gorton and Denton byelection, Labour’s national executive committee blocked him – in part because, they argued, Labour might find it hard to hold the Greater Manchester mayoralty in the byelection caused by his resignation. When he next applied to be a candidate, for Makerfield, the NEC no longer felt able t

stories.title: Trump’s Board of Peace drops full Gaza recovery plan in favour of tiny pilot scheme
stories.summary: Revised plan aims to ‘keep something going’ amid fears Netanyahu may gamble on new all-out offensive before Israeli elections The Gaza recovery plan being pursued by Donald Trump’s Board of Peace (BoP) has shrunk dramatically from an ambitious blueprint for the reconstruction of the whole territory to a small pilot project in the south of the strip. Even the envisaged pilot scheme – involving a temporary camp for a tiny fraction of Gaza’s 2 million displaced people, with a Palestinian administra

stories.title: Target of Monaco bomb blames attack on Ukrainian spy agency
stories.summary: Vadym Iermolaiev says military intelligence officers were behind assassination attempt that seriously wounded partner and child • Monaco bomb mystery deepens as Ukraine’s security services linked to murder of prime suspect The Ukrainian businessman who survived an assassination attempt in Monaco has accused Ukraine’s military intelligence agency of organising the attack. In his first public statement since the bombing that shook the wealthy principality, Vadym Iermolaiev alleged that serving and

stories.title: Mafia law gives Italian families right to break free from life of crime
stories.summary: Wives and children offered new identities to try to stop gangsters recruiting down the generations Children and young adults raised in mafia families will be given a chance to break away from organised crime under new legislation in Italy that aims to stop the intergenerational recruitment of gangsters. In an unprecedented effort to sever the family chain, the Italian state will offer children aged under 25 and other close relatives of mafia bosses a chance to start over: a new home in another c

stories.title: Former boss of Italian motorways sentenced to 12 years over Genoa bridge tragedy
stories.summary: Giovanni Castellucci among 32 people convicted over the 2018 Morandi Bridge collapse, which killed 43 Thirty-two people, including the former chief executive of Italy’s motorway operator, have been convicted over the 2018 collapse of a Genoa bridge in which 43 people died. In a hushed courtroom on Thursday in the north-western Italian city, Giovanni Castellucci, a former boss of Autostrade per l’Italia, was sentenced to 12 years in prison, the highest in the case. Continue reading...

stories.title: Donald Trump accuses China of meddling in US elections
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has again claimed he has evidence of interference in the 2020 presidential election, this time alleging China obtained 220 million voter records. China rejects the claim.

stories.title: China: Landslide buries buildings, traps people
stories.summary: An undetermined number of people have been reported trapped under a landslide in southwest China. Rescue efforts are ongoing.

stories.title: Iran's oil supply threat extends beyond Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Oil and gas producers in the Gulf are seeking alternatives to the Strait of Hormuz as traffic again comes under fire. But is Iran also turning its attention to the pipelines meant to bypass the waterway?

stories.title: How AI and satellites help fight wildfires
stories.summary: Wildfires are raging across Europe, Canada and beyond. To successfully fight these fires, speed is essential. A German startup uses satellite data and AI to spot fires early.

stories.title: The EU climate policy the world copied is under fire — what's at stake?
stories.summary: The EU Commission has proposed to weaken Europe's emissions trading system, a global blueprint for pricing carbon and cutting CO2. How effective is the scheme and where does it fall short?

stories.title: Japan: Superstitions make some homes hard to sell or rent
stories.summary: Many Japanese people are reluctant to buy or rent homes where a sudden or violent death has occurred. To reassure prospective buyers and tenants, property owners are calling in "ghost investigators."

stories.title: How new PM Andy Burnham will change UK's international role
stories.summary: From northwest England to the global stage: Andy Burnham becomes Britain's new prime minister on Monday. How will the former mayor of Manchester tackle international affairs?

stories.title: Fact check: Trump revives US election fraud claims
stories.summary: In a primetime address, US President Donald Trump alleged Chinese voter interference in the 2020 US election. DW examined the declassified documents behind his claims.

stories.title: Meet Andy Burnham, Britain's next prime minister
stories.summary: Andy Burnham was confirmed as Keir Starmer's successor as British prime minister. The former Manchester mayor became a leading figure in the Labour Party and one of the UK's most popular politicians.

stories.title: UK: Andy Burnham confirmed as Labour Party leader
stories.summary: The ruling center-left Labour Party has selected Andy Burnham as its new leader, clearing the way for him to replace Keir Starmer as British prime minister.

stories.title: Monaco assassination plot takes a new turn
stories.summary: A woman suspected of plotting to assassinate a Ukrainian-Cypriot businessman in Monaco has been found dead in Ukraine. Two men with ties to Ukraine's intelligence services have now been brought into custody.

stories.title: Iran war: US military attacks Iran for sixth night running
stories.summary: US Central Command said that a new wave of strikes was launched on Iran. It follows retaliatory strikes from Iran on sites in Jordan and Gulf states.

stories.title: Canada fires prompt US air quality alerts as smoke blankets major cities
stories.summary: Smoke from more than 800 blazes has filled major cities from Toronto and New York, to parts of the US Midwest and Great Lakes.

stories.title: Philippines condemns monkey video on Chinese media as racist
stories.summary: The AI video on China Daily showed a monkey in Philippine garments singing its claims to the South China Sea.

stories.title: Australia 'deeply frustrated' over Laos methanol poisoning charges
stories.summary: Media reports say Laos will press charges of up to one year in jail against those allegedly responsible.

stories.title: More than 500 Rohingya vanished at sea - what happened?
stories.summary: Two boats carrying an estimated 530 Rohingyas have disappeared since leaving Myanmar on 29 June.

stories.title: Olympic cyclist admits to driving despite ban after crash that killed wife
stories.summary: Rohan Dennis pleaded guilty to driving while disqualified after police pulled him over last month.

stories.title: Japan relaxes royal succession rules - but ban on female emperors remains
stories.summary: The law now allows the adoption of male distant relatives aged over 15 back into the imperial family.

stories.title: Trump Media to sell instant access to 'market-moving' social posts

stories.title: Új kihívás
Az állami adattörlés, amit senki nem kért, értelme se sok van, de legalább ki lehetett rá fizetni 31 milliárd forintot
stories.summary: A Miniszterelnökség feljelentést tett a véglegestörlés.hu miatt. A papíron jól hangzó megoldás a gyakorlatban túl bonyolult, alig hallott róla valaki, és fogalmunk sincs, hogy hányan használták egyáltalán, mert ezt még a hatóság sem tudja.

stories.title: Woman speaks out on husband being pulled from Ryanair plane – video

Within seconds, she saw her 61-year-old husband being sucked out of the plane through a dislodged cabin window. Karovic is now being treated in hospital in Thessaloniki with severe neck and arm injuries, Maksimovic said ‘If we die, we die together’: wife of Ryanair passenger almost sucked through window speaks Continue reading...

stories.title: How global heating supercharged floods in West Africa, displacing thousands
stories.summary: Adaptation to frightening new normal and reducing emissions further and faster is critical, scientists warn Dozens of people drowned, hundreds had to be rescued and thousands were displaced when floods struck the coasts of west Africa last month. Now scientists have concluded that the rains that caused the floods were supercharged by climate breakdown. Global heating, they say, turned what should have been a routine weather event into a climate catastrophe. Continue reading...

Copa Cogeca describes itself as the voice of 22 million farmers across the continent, and enjoys unrivalled access to EU lawmakers.

stories.title: Surviving extreme heat increasingly boils down to this: access to air conditioning | Mark Wolfe
stories.summary: The next great climate divide will be between countries that have the resources to adapt and those that don’t This summer, much of the media’s attention has focused on record temperatures across Europe and the United States. Television coverage has been filled with familiar images: heat maps shaded deep red, schools closing, rail lines slowing, wildfires spreading and emergency rooms treating growing numbers of people with heat-related illnesses. Public officials have responded with equally fami

stories.title: Ukraine parliament accepts PM Svyrydenko's resignation
stories.summary: With Ukraine "changing its political strategy," President Volodymyr Zelenskyy is trying to remake the government,

stories.title: T. rex 'Gus' sells for record-breaking $50.1 million
stories.summary: The Tyrannosaurus rex skeleton surpassed its pre-sale estimate and set a new auction record for a dinosaur fossil.

stories.title: Germany's Merz receives death threat from Iranian newspaper
stories.summary: In retaliation for Khamenei's death, a Tehran daily listed 13 world leaders, including German Chancellor Friedrich Merz, as targets. Politicians voiced concern.

stories.title: 150 years of the Bayreuth Festival: Confronting a troubled legacy
stories.summary: Should the past be confronted — or avoided? Why the Wagner festival's handling of its Nazi-era legacy remains a contentious issue in its 150th anniversary year.

stories.title: Why is Zelenskyy reshuffling Ukraine's government?
stories.summary: Ukraine's Prime Minister Yulia Svyrydenko has resigned, prompting a Cabinet reshuffle led by President Volodymyr Zelenskyy. What comes next?

stories.title: Why Indonesia's economy is under pressure
stories.summary: Southeast Asia's largest economy risks losing its emerging-market status as President Prabowo Subianto's spending plans rattle markets. Can he deliver growth?

stories.title: Running dry: How to store more groundwater for dry seasons
stories.summary: More frequent weather extremes are leading to water shortages during droughts and flooding during heavy rain. But groundwater storage can help secure water for dry seasons while mitigating extreme rain.

stories.title: Sweden prides itself on equality – so why is its political gender gap growing?
stories.summary: As general election looms, survey shows twice as many men as women support far-right Sweden Democrats One is led by Sweden’s first female prime minister, Magdalena Andersson , and has promised smaller school-class sizes, more housing and free dental care for young people. The other, led by Jimmie Åkesson , has neo-Nazi roots and has pledged to lower taxes, improve public safety and treat “anti-Swedishness” as a hate crime. In the run-up to Sweden’s general election in September, the Social Democ

stories.title: Protests engulf Indian state after rape and murder of 11-year-old girl
stories.summary: Innocent man lynched by mob in West Bengal as police killing of suspect further escalates tensions Protests have engulfed the Indian state of West Bengal after the rape and murder of an 11-year-old girl, the subsequent lynching of an innocent man and the police killing of one of the accused. Outrage erupted on Sunday after the body of a missing girl was recovered from a pond in a town just outside the state capital, Kolkata. Continue reading...

stories.title: France holds annual Bastille Day parade in Paris – video
stories.summary: France marked its annual Bastille Day celebration in Paris with a parade of military regiments, including foreign troops. Leaders from the 'coalition of the willing' were invited to watch the parade Continue reading...

stories.title: France opens terror probe after gun found in Jewish suburb
stories.summary: Police said they found a "military-grade weapon" during the search of a suspicious car parked in a Paris neighborhood with a large Jewish population.

stories.title: World Cup: Balogun red and expansion keep FIFA in spotlight
stories.summary: The fallout from Folarin Balogun's red card reversal continues as reports suggest the chairman of FIFA’s disciplinary committee made the decision unilaterally. Meanwhile, the World Cup could be set to expand once more.

stories.title: Germany set to restrict its Freedom of Information Act
stories.summary: Critics fear the reforms could weaken government transparency by curbing civil society's access to official information and limiting requests from foreigners.

stories.title: The week around the world in 20 pictures
stories.summary: The US’s 250th anniversary, the funeral of Ali Khamenei, another brutal heatwave in Europe and the World Cup – the past seven days as captured by the world’s leading photojournalists Continue reading...

stories.title: France and UK to increase staffing at border controls in effort to avert travel chaos
stories.summary: Disruption at Channel crossings expected to rise amid new fingerprint and facial recognition checks France and the UK have agreed to increase staffing at border controls in response to warnings of travel chaos caused by new fingerprinting and facial recognition checks. Disruption at Channel crossings is expected to rise sharply next weekend at the start of the summer holiday season, with MPs saying there would be “utter chaos and miles of tailbacks” unless the EU’s entry-exit system (EES) is fix

stories.title: New US Ebola patient arrives in Germany for treatment
stories.summary: Aid worker flown to Berlin as Trump administration bars Americans from traveling to US on commercial flights A US national who contracted Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo has arrived in Germany for treatment, the health ministry in Berlin said on Monday, weeks after another American infected with Ebola in the DRC was treated in Berlin. Meanwhile, the Trump administration on Monday said it was blocking American citizens in ⁠the DRC from traveling to the US on commercial flights, Reut

stories.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy swaps out prime minister in reshuffle
stories.summary: Ukraine's president announced a government shake up, with PM Yuliia Svyrydenko stepping down after a year in office.

stories.title: Sinner beats Zverev to retain Wimbledon title
stories.summary: Jannik Sinner retained his Wimbledon title by beating Alexander Zverev in Sunday's men's final. The Italian claims his fifth Grand Slam crown and extends his dominance over the German to 10 straight victories.

stories.title: EU approves new passenger rights for air travel
stories.summary: EU ministers have approved a set of reforms giving more rights to air travelers. Among other things, compensation claims are to be made easier.

stories.title: EU experts urge social media restrictions for kids under 13
stories.summary: An expert panel has recommended limiting access to social media for young children across the EU. The European Commission is expected to present a proposal for legislation on the issue in the coming months.

stories.title: Germany's Ahr Valley: Rebuilding better after deadly flood
stories.summary: New walls, redesigned bridges and restored floodplains: Towns in a small German valley are testing how they can survive the next extreme flood.

stories.title: France arrests arson suspects amid Fontainebleau forest fire
stories.summary: Firefighters are attempting to tackle a major fire burning since Sunday in the much-visited Fontainebleau forest, near France's capital Paris. Two people have been arrested for suspected arson.

stories.title: Micronutrients are good, a balanced diet is better
stories.summary: Chronic illnesses, heart disease, obesity ― we know eating too much of the wrong foods can be unhealthy. But what about "good" foods: Is modern farming diluting micronutrients in our fruit and vegetables?

stories.title: EU rejects suspending biometric border controls despite 20 ‘difficult spots’
stories.summary: Officials admit new post-Brexit EES system is ‘not perfect’, as airports and airlines voice fears over delays The EU has rejected calls by airports and airlines to suspend the implementation of new fingerprinting and facial recognition border controls even though it admits there are “20 difficult spots” with queue chaos. With only a week to go before the peak summer holiday season starts, EU officials said the new entry/exit system (EES) was “not perfect” but it would tell travel industry repres

stories.title: Pakistan locates wreckage of Boeing 737 cargo plane that went missing off coast
stories.summary: Early flight data shows K2 Airways plane crashed into sea with five crew on board south-west of Karachi Pakistan has located the wreckage of a Boeing cargo plane, the country’s airports authority said, adding that rescuers were searching for the five crew members on board when the aircraft went missing. The plane was approaching Karachi from Sharjah in the United Arab Emirates when radar showed it “rapidly descending” on Tuesday evening after reporting a “navigational system issue”, according to

stories.title: US charges Indian criminal gang leader with organising murder of Canadian Sikh activist
stories.summary: Lawrence Bishnoi, who is in prison in India, is accused of orchestrating assassination of Hardeep Singh Nijjar in 2023 US and Canadian authorities say they have “dismantled” the leadership of a notorious Indian criminal group, charging dozens of operatives who have “inflicted pain and cruelty on people, victims around the globe”, including a high-profile murder in Canada that strained diplomatic relations between Canada and India. At a press conference on Tuesday, members of the FBI and Canada’s

stories.title: Weather tracker: Typhoon leaves people stranded on rooftops in China
stories.summary: Eleven reported dead as flooding also brings danger of snakes, while buildings collapse in Mumbai amid heavy rain As the first typhoon to make landfall in China for the 2026 season, Maysak has caused devastating damage in southern and central regions. The Guangxi region received intense downpours of up to 280mm in 12 hours, causing rivers to swell and dam walls to break. By Monday morning, flooding across the city of Nanning and surrounding areas had resulted in many people being stranded on roo
